summary: Impacted Domains (context-system, workflow) are accurate and exhaustive for every In-Scope changed file. context-system OWNERSHIP.yaml paths:[internal/contextpack/**] owns internal/contextpack/spec.go (R1). workflow OWNERSHIP.yaml owns the rest: internal/validate/** (divergence.go, ownership.go, plan.go), internal/adr/** (parse.go test, R3), internal/approve/** (spec.go scaffoldPlan, R4), cmd/** (cmd/mindspec/adr.go, R3). Repo has exactly 4 domains (context-system, core, execution, workflow); checked core (internal/state,config,workspace,spec,...) and execution (internal/executor,bead,gitutil,safeio,harness) manifests — NO changed file falls under either, so no undeclared THIRD domain owns a changed file (the dogfooding trap is clear). Key check on R3: both workspace.FindRoot and FindLocalRoot already exist in internal/workspace/workspace.go, so the worktree-aware fix is confined to cmd/mindspec/adr.go calling the existing FindLocalRoot — no edit to internal/workspace/** (core domain) is required, so no spillover. R1's full-domain enumeration reuses the existing resolveDomains/attributeDomain machinery in internal/validate/{divergence,ownership}.go — both workflow-owned. ADR coverage OK: ADR-0024 (Accepted; Domains adr,context-system,workflow) covers BOTH impacted domains; ADR-0036 (Accepted; workflow,validation,doc-sync,ownership) covers workflow and is the on-point ZFC ownership-discovery decision R1 directly applies; ADR-0032 (Accepted; validation,adr,lifecycle) is genuinely relevant, not padding — it defines the divergence/coverage gate mechanism (checkADRCoverage, ValidateDivergence, the 'domain identifier is OWNERSHIP.yaml directory name' sub-decision 1, and the tri-state coverageAccepted predicate R1/R2/R4 operate inside). Spec's characterization of each ADR is accurate; note ADR-0036 was amended by spec 095 and ADR-0032 by PR #126 (tri-state) but both remain Accepted and the spec's reliance on coveredAccepted is consistent with the amendment. Self-consistency under R1: spec 100's own beads pass the divergence gate — every changed file resolves to context-system or workflow via glob-match, both are covered by cited Accepted ADR-0024, so NO self-block. domain identifier note: ADR-0032 sub-decision 1 mandates short-tag identifiers matching directory names, which is exactly what R1 preserves by treating Impacted Domains as advisory and resolving via paths: globs.
